evals.prompt: How do I structure a feature module in NestJS?
evals.expected_output: Create a directory with the feature name containing: feature.module.ts (imports, controllers, providers, exports), feature.controller.ts (HTTP routing only), feature.service.ts (business logic), dto/ directory (CreateDto, UpdateDto with class-validator), entities/ directory. The module exports only what other modules need via the exports array. Import the feature module in AppModule.

evals.prompt: What goes in a NestJS controller vs a service?
evals.expected_output: Controllers handle HTTP concerns only: routing (@Get, @Post), extracting params (@Param, @Body, @Query), setting status codes (@HttpCode), and calling services. No business logic in controllers. Services contain all business logic: database queries, validation rules, error throwing (NotFoundException, ConflictException). Controllers are thin HTTP adapters; services are the application core.

evals.prompt: How do I configure the NestJS app at startup — pipes, prefix, port?
evals.expected_output: In main.ts after NestFactory.create(AppModule): call app.useGlobalPipes(new ValidationPipe({ whitelist: true, transform: true, forbidNonWhitelisted: true })) for automatic DTO validation. Call app.setGlobalPrefix('api/v1') for a URL prefix. Call await app.listen(process.env.PORT ?? 3000). The ValidationPipe whitelist option strips unknown properties from request bodies.

should_not_trigger: How do I set up dependency injection in Angular services?
Configure Express middleware for a Node.js API
Set up Next.js Route Handlers with authentication
